provenance: Original MCQs synthesized from the local AWS-first knowledge base and linked official AWS documentation.
redistribution_note: The raw Udemy mock files remain outside public/data and are not read by this generator.

items.prompt: An application must use one conversational request structure across several supported Amazon Bedrock chat models. Which API is the best default?

choices.text: Converse
choices.rationale: Correct. Converse provides a common message interface for supported conversational models.

choices.text: InvokeModel with one identical JSON body for every model
choices.rationale: Incorrect. InvokeModel request bodies remain model and provider specific.

choices.text: SageMaker InvokeEndpoint
choices.rationale: Incorrect. This is for a deployed SageMaker endpoint, not managed Bedrock chat models.

choices.text: Amazon SQS SendMessage
choices.rationale: Incorrect. SQS decouples work but is not a foundation-model inference API.

items.explanation: Correct. Converse provides a common message interface for supported conversational models.

items.prompt: One required Bedrock model serves interactive traffic in a fixed Region. Demand rises predictably every weekday and sustained throttling occurs. Which capacity choice best fits?

choices.text: Provisioned Throughput sized from token and request demand
choices.rationale: Correct. Predictable sustained demand for one required model is the main provisioned-capacity case.

choices.text: Unbounded SDK retries
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Retries amplify sustained overload and do not create capacity.

choices.text: Cross-Region inference without checking residency
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Cross-Region routing addresses eligible regional capacity and resilience, not a fixed-Region requirement.

choices.text: A larger CloudWatch log group
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Logging capacity does not change model inference capacity.

items.explanation: Correct. Predictable sustained demand for one required model is the main provisioned-capacity case.

items.prompt: A runbook assistant answers natural-language symptom queries well but misses exact error codes that exist in indexed chunks. What should be changed first?

choices.text: Use hybrid lexical and vector retrieval, then rerank a sufficient candidate set
choices.rationale: Correct. Lexical retrieval preserves exact tokens while vector retrieval covers semantic symptoms.

choices.text: Increase model temperature
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Generation randomness cannot recover candidates that retrieval omitted.

choices.text: Replace every chunk with a much larger chunk
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Larger chunks can reduce retrieval precision and do not guarantee exact-token matching.

choices.text: Rerank before retrieving candidates
choices.rationale: Incorrect. A reranker can only reorder candidates already retrieved.

items.explanation: Correct. Lexical retrieval preserves exact tokens while vector retrieval covers semantic symptoms.

sources.title: Neural and hybrid search in OpenSearch Serverless

items.source_title: Neural and hybrid search in OpenSearch Serverless

items.prompt: Source documents are replaced and deleted during the day, but a knowledge base sometimes cites obsolete versions. Which action directly addresses freshness?

choices.text: Trigger or schedule ingestion and verify the job reaches terminal success
choices.rationale: Correct. Source changes are not current in retrieval until synchronization completes successfully.

choices.text: Fine-tune the foundation model after every document change
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Frequently changing factual content belongs in synchronized retrieval, not repeated model training.

choices.text: Increase output token limits
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Output capacity does not update indexed source content.

choices.text: Hide citations
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Removing evidence conceals the symptom rather than fixing stale retrieval.

items.explanation: Correct. Source changes are not current in retrieval until synchronization completes successfully.

items.prompt: A shared production prompt needs reusable versions, approval evidence, regression testing, staged promotion, and rapid rollback. Which TWO controls are required?

choices.text: Store immutable prompt versions in Bedrock Prompt Management
choices.rationale: Correct. Versioned prompt artifacts provide stable references and rollback targets.

choices.text: Use a release pipeline with tests and an explicit approval gate
choices.rationale: Correct. Organizational promotion and approval remain external release controls.

choices.text: Treat creation of a prompt version as automatic production approval
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Versioning alone does not prove review or promotion authorization.

choices.text: Edit the production prompt in place without retaining the previous version
choices.rationale: Incorrect. This removes traceability and a safe rollback target.

choices.text: Test only whether the prompt is grammatically correct
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Regression tests must validate required behavior and output contracts.

items.explanation: Correct. Versioned prompt artifacts provide stable references and rollback targets. Correct. Organizational promotion and approval remain external release controls.

items.prompt: A prompt update improves tone but occasionally omits a required JSON field. What is the strongest release control?

choices.text: Validate the schema on a fixed regression set and block promotion when the contract fails
choices.rationale: Correct. Deterministic schema checks should gate a required machine-readable contract.

choices.text: Lower temperature and deploy immediately
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Lower randomness cannot guarantee a required field and does not provide release evidence.

choices.text: Increase the model's output limit
choices.rationale: Incorrect. More output capacity does not enforce the JSON schema.

choices.text: Ask users to retry malformed responses
choices.rationale: Incorrect. This shifts a preventable contract failure to users.

items.explanation: Correct. Deterministic schema checks should gate a required machine-readable contract.

items.prompt: A high-risk agent workflow sometimes needs an engineer approval that can take two days. Which pattern preserves state without keeping compute running?

choices.text: A Step Functions Standard callback task with a task token
choices.rationale: Correct. The workflow can pause durably and resume from a trusted callback.

choices.text: A Lambda function that sleeps until approval
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Lambda is not designed to wait for a multi-day human decision.

choices.text: A model loop that repeatedly asks whether approval arrived
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Polling wastes tokens and leaves approval control to a probabilistic component.

choices.text: An SNS notification with no stored workflow state
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Notification alone does not preserve or resume the controlled workflow.

items.explanation: Correct. The workflow can pause durably and resume from a trusted callback.

items.prompt: An agent repeatedly calls a failing dependency and consumes tokens without progress. Which TWO controls most directly stop the failure pattern?

choices.text: A maximum cycle count with an explicit terminal state
choices.rationale: Correct. A hard bound prevents an unlimited reason-act loop.

choices.text: A circuit breaker that opens after repeated dependency failures
choices.rationale: Correct. The breaker prevents calls while the dependency remains unhealthy.

choices.text: An unlimited retry policy
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Unlimited retries worsen token waste and dependency pressure.

choices.text: Broader tool permissions
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Additional permissions do not repair dependency health and increase risk.

choices.text: A larger model context window
choices.rationale: Incorrect. More context does not create a stop condition.

items.explanation: Correct. A hard bound prevents an unlimited reason-act loop. Correct. The breaker prevents calls while the dependency remains unhealthy.

items.prompt: Nightly JSON records in S3 must satisfy required-field and non-empty-value rules before inference. Failed records need quarantine and operational metrics. Which service is the best fit?

choices.text: AWS Glue Data Quality in the processing job
choices.rationale: Correct. Managed rules can enforce, quarantine, and publish recurring data-quality results.

choices.text: Manual inspection in SageMaker Data Wrangler every night
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Interactive manual preparation does not meet recurring automated enforcement.

choices.text: A foundation model asked to guess missing values
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Probabilistic inference is not a deterministic data-quality gate.

choices.text: CloudTrail event history
choices.rationale: Incorrect. CloudTrail audits API activity but does not validate record completeness.

items.explanation: Correct. Managed rules can enforce, quarantine, and publish recurring data-quality results.

items.prompt: A workflow must extract tables from scanned forms and transcribe recorded calls. Which TWO managed services match the modalities?

choices.text: Amazon Textract for document text, forms, and tables
choices.rationale: Correct. Textract extracts structured content from documents.

choices.text: Amazon Transcribe for speech-to-text
choices.rationale: Correct. Transcribe processes recorded or streaming speech.

choices.text: Amazon Macie for audio transcription
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Macie discovers sensitive data in S3 objects; it is not a speech recognizer.

choices.text: Amazon Route 53 for table extraction
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Route 53 provides DNS and routing, not document extraction.

choices.text: AWS WAF for optical character recognition
choices.rationale: Incorrect. WAF filters web requests and does not perform OCR.

items.explanation: Correct. Textract extracts structured content from documents. Correct. Transcribe processes recorded or streaming speech.

items.prompt: Sensitive identifiers must be detected and masked in live user text before it reaches a model. Which choice best fits the real-time requirement?

choices.text: Runtime preprocessing with Comprehend PII detection or supported Guardrail PII controls
choices.rationale: Correct. These controls can act on live text before or during the inference path.

choices.text: Amazon Macie as a synchronous chat interceptor
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Macie is for sensitive-data discovery in S3, not live prompt interception.

choices.text: CloudTrail after the prompt has already reached the model
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Audit evidence after exposure is not preventive redaction.

choices.text: A larger context window
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Context size does not protect sensitive data.

items.explanation: Correct. These controls can act on live text before or during the inference path.

items.prompt: A chatbot must return a customer's latest five transaction amounts without fabrication and must never execute write SQL. Which design is safest?

choices.text: Use the model only for intent mapping, then authorize and run an approved parameterized read-only query in application code
choices.rationale: Correct. Deterministic code owns authorization, data access, and exact financial values.

choices.text: Let the model generate and execute arbitrary SQL
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Free-form generated SQL is not a safe authorization or write boundary.

choices.text: Ask the model to remember prior transaction values
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Model memory is not the authoritative transaction system.

choices.text: Use Guardrails alone to guarantee exact amounts
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Guardrails address supported safety policies, not deterministic database correctness.

items.explanation: Correct. Deterministic code owns authorization, data access, and exact financial values.

items.prompt: A private workload must invoke Bedrock without public internet access and only from an approved application role. Which TWO controls are necessary?

choices.text: A supported interface VPC endpoint with restrictive endpoint and network policies
choices.rationale: Correct. The endpoint provides a private service path and can restrict use.

choices.text: Least-privilege IAM permission for the approved model and actions
choices.rationale: Correct. Private connectivity does not replace authorization.

choices.text: A public NAT gateway as the only path
choices.rationale: Incorrect. That does not satisfy the no-public-path requirement.

choices.text: No IAM policy because the endpoint is private
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Network location alone does not authorize service actions.

choices.text: Amazon Macie attached to every runtime request
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Macie does not establish Bedrock network connectivity or runtime authorization.

items.explanation: Correct. The endpoint provides a private service path and can restrict use. Correct. Private connectivity does not replace authorization.

items.prompt: Employees need federated workforce access to AWS accounts and permission sets. Which identity service is the direct fit?

choices.text: IAM Identity Center
choices.rationale: Correct. It manages workforce access, groups, and permission sets across AWS accounts.

choices.text: Amazon Cognito user pools
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Cognito primarily provides application end-user identity, not workforce AWS permission sets.

choices.text: AWS WAF
choices.rationale: Incorrect. WAF filters web requests and is not a workforce identity provider.

choices.text: Amazon S3 Lifecycle
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Lifecycle manages object retention and storage transitions.

items.explanation: Correct. It manages workforce access, groups, and permission sets across AWS accounts.

items.prompt: Three models and two prompts must be compared fairly on quality, latency, tokens, and human preference. What should the team do first?

choices.text: Run every candidate combination against the same versioned representative benchmark
choices.rationale: Correct. A fixed dataset and controlled settings make comparisons attributable.

choices.text: Give each model a different convenient sample
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Different samples confound the comparison.

choices.text: Select the model with the lowest token price before measuring quality
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Price alone ignores retries, escalation, rework, and business effectiveness.

choices.text: Deploy every candidate to all users immediately
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Offline evaluation should precede a limited production canary.

items.explanation: Correct. A fixed dataset and controlled settings make comparisons attributable.

items.prompt: A RAG assistant produces unsupported answers. Which test best isolates whether candidate retrieval is the problem?

choices.text: Run retrieve-only evaluation and inspect content, metadata, rank, and coverage
choices.rationale: Correct. Removing generation isolates retrieval quality.

choices.text: Change the model, prompt, index, and chunking in one experiment
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Multiple simultaneous changes destroy causal attribution.

choices.text: Measure only API availability
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Availability cannot show whether retrieved evidence is relevant.

choices.text: Increase answer temperature
choices.rationale: Incorrect. More randomness cannot diagnose missing evidence.

items.explanation: Correct. Removing generation isolates retrieval quality.

items.prompt: A chat application has a large stable prompt prefix and users wait too long before seeing the first token. Quality and the model must remain unchanged. Which TWO changes directly help?

choices.text: Use prompt caching for the eligible stable prefix
choices.rationale: Correct. Prompt caching can avoid repeated processing of eligible shared prefix content.

choices.text: Stream output and measure time to first token separately
choices.rationale: Correct. Streaming reduces perceived wait even when total generation time is similar.

choices.text: Increase maximum output tokens substantially
choices.rationale: Incorrect. A larger ceiling can increase capacity and cost exposure.

choices.text: Add unlimited retries
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Retries can increase latency and overload.

choices.text: Disable all latency metrics
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Removing evidence makes optimization harder.

items.explanation: Correct. Prompt caching can avoid repeated processing of eligible shared prefix content. Correct. Streaming reduces perceived wait even when total generation time is similar.

items.prompt: Request count is stable, but tenants send prompts of very different sizes and intermittently throttle. What is the best admission-control unit?

choices.text: Estimated input tokens plus output budget, enforced by a per-tenant token bucket
choices.rationale: Correct. Token demand better represents model capacity than request count.

choices.text: Only requests per minute
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Equal request counts can represent very different token demand.

choices.text: CloudTrail event count
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Audit events are not an inference-capacity unit.

choices.text: Number of S3 objects
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Stored object count does not measure an assembled runtime request.

items.explanation: Correct. Token demand better represents model capacity than request count.

items.prompt: An auditor asks who changed an AWS resource, while an engineer asks why one request was slow. Which mapping is correct?

choices.text: CloudTrail for API audit; X-Ray and correlated metrics for request-path latency
choices.rationale: Correct. These services answer different evidence questions.

choices.text: X-Ray for identity audit; CloudTrail for model quality
choices.rationale: Incorrect. X-Ray traces request paths, and CloudTrail does not evaluate semantic quality.

choices.text: CloudWatch Synthetics for every historical API change
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Canaries test deployed paths; they are not the authoritative API audit.

choices.text: One raw prompt log for both requirements
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Raw payload logging creates privacy risk and still may not isolate a distributed latency segment.

items.explanation: Correct. These services answer different evidence questions.

items.prompt: A GenAI API has latency spikes across retrieval, model invocation, and tools. What evidence design best isolates the slow component?

choices.text: A correlation ID, structured component metrics, and distributed trace segments
choices.rationale: Correct. Correlated evidence separates retrieval, model, tool, and application latency.

choices.text: Only a final total-latency number
choices.rationale: Incorrect. End-to-end latency does not identify the responsible component.

choices.text: Unstructured error strings without request identity
choices.rationale: Incorrect. These cannot reliably join events across services.

choices.text: Provider-internal chain-of-thought logs
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Internal reasoning is not the supported operational trace and should not be exposed.

items.explanation: Correct. Correlated evidence separates retrieval, model, tool, and application latency.

items.prompt: A legacy HTTPS webhook needs acknowledgment within two seconds, traffic is bursty, and model output can update a CRM later. Which architecture best fits?

choices.text: API Gateway validates and enqueues to SQS; workers process asynchronously with idempotent CRM updates
choices.rationale: Correct. The queue decouples acknowledgment from slow work and absorbs bursts.

choices.text: Keep the webhook open until the model and CRM both finish
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Slow downstream work violates the acknowledgment constraint.

choices.text: Use EventBridge as a worker-consumed backlog with no queue
choices.rationale: Incorrect. EventBridge routes events, while SQS supplies backlog and worker backpressure.

choices.text: Retry CRM writes without an idempotency key
choices.rationale: Incorrect. At-least-once delivery can duplicate side effects.

items.explanation: Correct. The queue decouples acknowledgment from slow work and absorbs bursts.

items.prompt: A producer must publish one business event and allow new rule-based consumers to be added without changing producer code. Which service is the best fit?

choices.text: Amazon EventBridge
choices.rationale: Correct. Event rules and targets support decoupled routing and fanout.

choices.text: Amazon SQS as the only destination
choices.rationale: Incorrect. SQS is strongest for backlog and worker consumption, not changing rule-based fanout by itself.

choices.text: A direct synchronous call to every consumer
choices.rationale: Incorrect. This tightly couples the producer to current consumers.

choices.text: Amazon EBS
choices.rationale: Incorrect. EBS provides block storage, not event routing.

items.explanation: Correct. Event rules and targets support decoupled routing and fanout.

items.prompt: A 70 GB open-weight model on a SageMaker real-time endpoint fails while downloading and loading, and the initial storage is too small. Which TWO corrections address the stated causes?

choices.text: Provide sufficient endpoint storage for model artifacts and runtime files
choices.rationale: Correct. The model cannot load when the attached storage is insufficient.

choices.text: Set model-download and startup-health timeouts to realistic values after sizing resources
choices.rationale: Correct. Large models can require longer legitimate download and initialization time.

choices.text: Only increase retries from the client
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Client retries do not repair endpoint storage or startup configuration.

choices.text: Reduce CloudTrail retention
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Audit retention does not change model loading.

choices.text: Assume any GPU topology can hold the model
choices.rationale: Incorrect. GPU memory and serving topology must also be validated.

items.explanation: Correct. The model cannot load when the attached storage is insufficient. Correct. Large models can require longer legitimate download and initialization time.

items.prompt: A required open-weight model is not available through Bedrock, and the team needs a custom container, accelerator selection, and serving configuration. Which platform is the stronger fit?

choices.text: Amazon SageMaker AI
choices.rationale: Correct. SageMaker provides control over model artifacts, containers, accelerators, endpoints, and scaling.

choices.text: Bedrock solely because it has lower operational overhead
choices.rationale: Incorrect. Managed simplicity cannot satisfy a model and serving stack that Bedrock does not support.

choices.text: Amazon Route 53
choices.rationale: Incorrect. DNS routing does not host the model.

choices.text: AWS CloudTrail
choices.rationale: Incorrect. CloudTrail records API activity and is not an inference platform.

items.explanation: Correct. SageMaker provides control over model artifacts, containers, accelerators, endpoints, and scaling.

sources.title: Deploy models for inference with SageMaker AI
